title: How to COUNTIF across multiple columns
task: Count matches in a whole grid, not just one column.
keywords: count value in table google sheets

excel.note: COUNTIF happily takes a 2-D range — this is all it needs.

explanation: COUNTIF's range can be any rectangle — it scans every cell in the 3×3 grid and finds four "yes" values. The limitation people then hit: COUNTIFS requires all its criteria ranges to be the SAME shape, so you can't mix a 2-D range with a 1-D condition. For "count rows where ANY column says yes", use =SUMPRODUCT(--(COUNTIF(OFFSET(...)...))) patterns or simply add per-column COUNTIFS. Wildcards and comparisons work exactly as in the single-column case.
